client.c(3) Library Functions Manual client.c(3)

NAME

client.c - Client-side handshake protocol implementation.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Client: Process server's public key and send our public key. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_auth_response (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Client: Process auth challenge and send response. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_complete (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, acip_transport_t *transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t *payload, size_t payload_len)
Client: Wait for handshake complete confirmation. asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t client_socket)
Legacy wrapper: Key exchange using socket (TCP clients only). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t client_socket)
Legacy wrapper: Auth response using socket (TCP clients only). asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t *ctx, socket_t client_socket)
Legacy wrapper: Complete handshake using socket (TCP clients only).

Detailed Description

Client-side handshake protocol implementation.

Definition in file lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_auth_response (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport, packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t payload_len)

Client: Process auth challenge and send response.

Parameters

ctx Handshake context (must be in CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE state)
transport ACIP transport to send on
packet_type Packet type received (should be PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE or PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE)
payload Received packet payload
payload_len Length of received payload

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure

Client processes server's AUTH_CHALLENGE packet and sends AUTH_RESPONSE. Generates HMAC bound to shared secret using password or client key.

Note

Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling this function

State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE -> CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING

Definition at line 611 of file lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.

613                                                                             {
614   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE) {
615     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", ctx, ctx ? ctx->state : -1);
616   }
617   if (!transport) {
618     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
619   }
620 
621   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
622   int result;
623 
624   // If server sent HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, authentication was skipped (client has
625   // no key)
626   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE) {
627     ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
628     ctx->crypto_ctx.handshake_complete = true; // Mark crypto context as ready for rekeying
629     log_debug("Crypto handshake completed successfully (no authentication required)");
630     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
631   }
632 
633   // If server sent AUTH_FAILED, client is not authorized
634   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED) {
635     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server rejected authentication - client key not authorized");
636   }
637 
638   // Otherwise, verify packet type is AUTH_CHALLENGE
639   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE) {
640     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
641                      "Expected AUTH_CHALLENGE, HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, or AUTH_FAILED, "
642                      "got packet type %d",
643                      packet_type);
644   }
645 
646   // Validate packet size using session parameters
647   asciichat_error_t validation_result =
648       crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE, payload_len);
649   if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
650     return validation_result;
651   }
652 
653   // Parse auth requirement flags
654   uint8_t auth_flags = payload[0];
655 
656   // Copy nonce to local buffer before freeing payload
657   // Use auth_challenge_size since that's what the server sent
658   // Note: auth_challenge_size is uint8_t (max 255), buffer is 256 bytes, so always sufficient
659   uint8_t nonce_buffer[256];
660   memcpy(nonce_buffer, payload + 1, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_challenge_size);
661   const uint8_t *nonce = nonce_buffer;
662 
663   log_debug("Server auth requirements: password=%s, client_key=%s",
664             (auth_flags & AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD) ? "required" : "no",
665             (auth_flags & AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY) ? "required" : "no");
666 
667   // Check if we can satisfy the server's authentication requirements
668   bool has_password = ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password;
669   bool has_client_key = (ctx->client_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
670   bool password_required = (auth_flags & AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD);
671   bool client_key_required = (auth_flags & AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY);
672 
673   // Provide specific error messages based on what's missing
674   if (password_required && !has_password) {
675     if (client_key_required && !has_client_key) {
676       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server requires both password and client key authentication. Please "
677                                      "provide --password and --key to authenticate");
678     }
679     // Prompt for password interactively
680     char prompted_password[PASSWORD_BUFFER_SIZE];
681     if (prompt_password("Server password required - please enter password:", prompted_password,
682                         sizeof(prompted_password)) != 0) {
683       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to read password");
684     }
685 
686     // Derive password key from prompted password
687     log_debug("Deriving key from prompted password");
688     crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_derive_password_key(&ctx->crypto_ctx, prompted_password);
689     sodium_memzero(prompted_password, sizeof(prompted_password));
690 
691     if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
692       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to derive password key: %s", crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
693     }
694 
695     // Mark that password auth is now available
696     ctx->crypto_ctx.has_password = true;
697     has_password = true; // Update flag for logic below
698   }
699 
700   // Authentication response priority:
701   // NOTE: Identity verification happens during KEY_EXCHANGE phase, not
702   // AUTH_RESPONSE!
703   // 1. If server requires password → MUST send HMAC (hmac_size bytes), error if no password
704   // 2. Else if server requires identity (whitelist) → MUST send Ed25519 signature (signature_size bytes), error if no
705   // key
706   // 3. Else if client has password → send HMAC (optional password auth)
707   // 4. Else if client has SSH key → send Ed25519 signature (optional identity
708   // proof)
709   // 5. Else → no authentication available
710 
711   // Clean up payload before any early returns
712   if (password_required) {
713     // Server requires password - HIGHEST PRIORITY
714     // (Identity was already verified in KEY_EXCHANGE phase if whitelist is
715     // enabled)
716     if (!has_password) {
717       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server requires password authentication\n"
718                                      "Please provide --password for this server");
719     }
720 
721     result = send_password_auth_response(ctx, transport, nonce, "required password");
722     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
723       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send password auth response");
724       return result;
725     }
726   } else if (client_key_required) {
727     // Server requires client key (whitelist) - SECOND PRIORITY
728     if (!has_client_key) {
729       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server requires client key authentication (whitelist)\n"
730                                      "Please provide --key with your authorized Ed25519 key");
731     }
732 
733     result = send_key_auth_response(ctx, transport, nonce, "required client key");
734     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
735       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send key auth response");
736       return result;
737     }
738   } else if (has_password) {
739     // No server requirements, but client has password → send HMAC + client
740     // nonce (optional)
741     result = send_password_auth_response(ctx, transport, nonce, "optional password");
742     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
743       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send password auth response");
744       return result;
745     }
746   } else if (has_client_key) {
747     // No server requirements, but client has SSH key → send Ed25519 signature +
748     // client nonce (optional)
749     result = send_key_auth_response(ctx, transport, nonce, "optional identity");
750     if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
751       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send key auth response");
752       return result;
753     }
754   } else {
755     // No authentication method available
756     // Continue without authentication (server will decide if this is
757     // acceptable)
758     log_debug("No authentication credentials provided - continuing without "
759               "authentication");
760   }
761 
762   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING;
763 
764   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
765 }




References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_context_t::auth_challenge_size,
    AUTH_REQUIRE_CLIENT_KEY, AUTH_REQUIRE_PASSWORD,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    crypto_derive_password_key(), CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    ERROR_NETWORK, ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
    crypto_context_t::handshake_complete,
    crypto_context_t::has_password, KEY_TYPE_ED25519,
    log_debug, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_CHALLENGE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, PASSWORD_BUFFER_SIZE,
    prompt_password(), SET_ERRNO,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, and
  private_key_t::type.


Referenced by client_handle_auth_challenge(), and
    crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket().






asciichat_error_t
  crypto_handshake_client_auth_response_socket
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t client_socket)


Legacy wrapper: Auth response using socket (TCP clients only).


Deprecated


Use crypto_handshake_client_auth_response() with
  acip_transport_t instead


Definition at line 908 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


909                                                                                        {
910   // Receive packet using old method
911   packet_type_t packet_type;
912   uint8_t *payload = NULL;
913   size_t payload_len = 0;
914   int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
915   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
916     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive packet from server");
917   }
918 
919   // Create temporary TCP transport
920   acip_transport_t *temp_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("crypto_handshake_temp_socket", client_socket, NULL);
921   if (!temp_transport) {
922     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create temporary transport");
923   }
924 
925   // Call new function (takes ownership of payload and will free it)
926   asciichat_error_t handshake_result =
927       crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(ctx, temp_transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
928 
929   // Destroy temporary transport
930   acip_transport_destroy(temp_transport);
931 
932   return handshake_result;
933 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_client_auth_response(), ERROR_NETWORK,
    receive_packet(), and SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by client_crypto_handshake().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_complete
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport,
  packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t
  payload_len)


Client: Wait for handshake complete confirmation.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be in
  CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING state)


transport ACIP transport (unused, for consistency)


packet_type Packet type received (should be
  PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP or PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED)


payload Received packet payload


payload_len Length of received payload


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Client processes server's SERVER_AUTH_RESP or AUTH_FAILED packet.
    After receiving, handshake is complete and encryption is ready.




Note


Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling
  this function



State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING ->
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY




Definition at line 767 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


769                                                                        {
770   // Accept both KEY_EXCHANGE and AUTHENTICATING states for simple mode compatibility
771   // In simple mode, server skips AUTH_CHALLENGE and sends HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE directly
772   if (!ctx || (ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE && ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING)) {
773     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", ctx, ctx ? ctx->state : -1);
774     return ERROR_INVALID_STATE;
775   }
776   if (!transport) {
777     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
778   }
779 
780   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
781   (void)transport; // Unused parameter (this function only receives, doesn't send)
782 
783   // Check packet type
784   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED) {
785     // Parse the auth failure packet to get specific reasons
786     if (payload_len >= sizeof(auth_failure_packet_t)) {
787       auth_failure_packet_t *failure = (auth_failure_packet_t *)payload;
788       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Server rejected authentication:");
789 
790       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT) {
791         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Incorrect password");
792       }
793       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_REQUIRED) {
794         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Server requires a password (use --password)");
795       }
796       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED) {
797         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Server requires a whitelisted client key (use --key "
798                                      "with your SSH key)");
799       }
800       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED) {
801         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Your client key is not in the server's whitelist");
802       }
803       if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_SIGNATURE_INVALID) {
804         SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "  - Client signature verification failed");
805       }
806 
807       // Provide helpful guidance
808       if (failure->reason_flags & (AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT | AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED)) {
809         if ((failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT) &&
810             (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED)) {
811           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Hint: Server requires BOTH correct password AND "
812                                        "whitelisted key");
813         } else if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT) {
814           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Hint: Check your password and try again");
815         } else if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED) {
816           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Hint: Provide your SSH key with --key ~/.ssh/id_ed25519");
817         } else if (failure->reason_flags & AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED) {
818           SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Hint: Your key needs to be added to the server's whitelist");
819         }
820       }
821     } else {
822       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "Server rejected authentication (no details provided)");
823     }
824     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH,
825                      "Server authentication failed - incorrect HMAC"); // Special code for
826                                                                        // auth failure - do
827                                                                        // not retry
828   }
829 
830   // Handle no-auth flow: server sends HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE directly
831   if (packet_type == PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE) {
832     ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
833     log_info("Handshake complete (no authentication required)");
834     return ASCIICHAT_OK;
835   }
836 
837   // Handle with-auth flow: server sends SERVER_AUTH_RESP after authentication
838   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP) {
839     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
840                      "Expected HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE, SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE, or AUTH_FAILED, got packet type %d",
841                      packet_type);
842   }
843 
844   // Verify server's HMAC for mutual authentication
845   // Use ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size (negotiated during handshake) rather than SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE_SIZE constant
846   if (payload_len != ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size) {
847     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Invalid SERVER_AUTH_RESPONSE size: %zu bytes (expected %u)", payload_len,
848                      ctx->crypto_ctx.hmac_size);
849   }
850 
851   // Verify server's HMAC (binds to DH shared_secret to prevent MITM)
852   if (!crypto_verify_auth_response(&ctx->crypto_ctx, ctx->client_challenge_nonce, payload)) {
853     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "SECURITY: Server authentication failed - incorrect HMAC");
854     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH, "This may indicate a man-in-the-middle attack!");
855     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH,
856                      "Server authentication failed - incorrect HMAC"); // Authentication
857                                                                        // failure - do not
858                                                                        // retry
859   }
860   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY;
861   log_info("Server authentication successful - mutual authentication complete");
862 
863   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
864 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REJECTED, AUTH_FAIL_CLIENT_KEY_REQUIRED,
    AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_INCORRECT, AUTH_FAIL_PASSWORD_REQUIRED,
    AUTH_FAIL_SIGNATURE_INVALID,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_challenge_nonce,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_AUTHENTICATING,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_READY,
    crypto_verify_auth_response(), ERROR_CRYPTO_AUTH,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, crypto_context_t::hmac_size,
    log_info, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_AUTH_FAILED,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_COMPLETE,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_SERVER_AUTH_RESP,
    auth_failure_packet_t::reason_flags, SET_ERRNO, and
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state.

    crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_complete_socket
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, socket_t client_socket)


Legacy wrapper: Complete handshake using socket (TCP clients
    only).


Deprecated


Use crypto_handshake_client_complete() with
  acip_transport_t instead


Definition at line 938 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


938                                                                                                                    {
939   // Receive packet using old method
940   packet_type_t packet_type;
941   uint8_t *payload = NULL;
942   size_t payload_len = 0;
943   int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
944   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
945     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive handshake completion packet");
946   }
947 
948   // Create temporary TCP transport
949   acip_transport_t *temp_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("crypto_handshake_temp_socket", client_socket, NULL);
950   if (!temp_transport) {
951     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create temporary transport");
952   }
953 
954   // Call new function (takes ownership of payload and will free it)
955   asciichat_error_t handshake_result =
956       crypto_handshake_client_complete(ctx, temp_transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
957 
958   // Destroy temporary transport
959   acip_transport_destroy(temp_transport);
960 
961   return handshake_result;
962 }

    crypto_handshake_client_complete(), ERROR_NETWORK,

Referenced by client_crypto_handshake().






asciichat_error_t crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange
  (crypto_handshake_context_t * ctx, acip_transport_t * transport,
  packet_type_t packet_type, const uint8_t * payload, size_t
  payload_len)


Client: Process server's public key and send our public key.


Parameters


ctx Handshake context (must be in
  CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT state)


transport ACIP transport to send on


packet_type Packet type received (should be
  PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT)


payload Received packet payload


payload_len Length of received payload


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Client processes server's KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packet and responds
    with KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP. Supports both simple and authenticated formats.
    Verifies server signature if present.




Note


Packet must be received by ACIP handler before calling
  this function



State transition: CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT ->
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE




Definition at line 31 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


33                                                                            {
34   if (!ctx || ctx->state != CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT) {
35     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Invalid state: ctx=%p, state=%d", (void *)ctx, ctx ? (int)ctx->state : -1);
36   }
37   if (!transport) {
38     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "transport is NULL");
39   }
40 
41   // Note: Packet already received by ACIP handler
42   int result;
43 
44   // Verify packet type
45   if (packet_type != PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT) {
46     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL, "Expected KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, got packet type %d", packet_type);
47   }
48 
49   log_debug("CLIENT_KEY_EXCHANGE: Received packet with payload_len=%zu, kex_size=%u, auth_size=%u, sig_size=%u",
50             payload_len, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size,
51             ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size);
52 
53   // Check payload size - only authenticated format supported
54   // Authenticated: public_key_size + auth_public_key_size + signature_size bytes
55   size_t expected_auth_size =
56       ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size;
57 
58   uint8_t *server_ephemeral_key;
59   // Use the crypto context's public key size to ensure compatibility
60   size_t key_size = sizeof(ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key);
61   server_ephemeral_key = SAFE_MALLOC(key_size, uint8_t *);
62   if (!server_ephemeral_key) {
63     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for server ephemeral key");
64   }
65   uint8_t *server_identity_key;
66   server_identity_key = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size, uint8_t *);
67   uint8_t *server_signature;
68   server_signature = SAFE_MALLOC(ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size, uint8_t *);
69 
70   if (!server_identity_key || !server_signature) {
71     SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
72     if (server_identity_key)
73       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
74     if (server_signature)
75       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
76     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate memory for server identity key or signature");
77   }
78 
79   // Validate packet size using session parameters
80   asciichat_error_t validation_result =
81       crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(ctx, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT, payload_len);
82   if (validation_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
83     SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
84     SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
85     SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
86     return validation_result;
87   }
88 
89   // Check if server is using authentication (auth_public_key_size > 0 means
90   // authenticated format)
91   if (ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size > 0 && payload_len == expected_auth_size) {
92     // Authenticated format:
93     // [ephemeral:public_key_size][identity:auth_public_key_size][signature:signature_size]
94     log_debug("Received authenticated KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT (%zu bytes)", expected_auth_size);
95     memcpy(server_ephemeral_key, payload, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
96     memcpy(server_identity_key, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size);
97     memcpy(server_signature, payload + ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size,
98            ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size);
99 
100     // Server is using client authentication
101     ctx->server_uses_client_auth = true;
102 
103     // DEBUG: Print identity key received
104     char hex_id[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
105     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
106       safe_snprintf(hex_id + i * 2, 3, "%02x", server_identity_key[i]);
107     }
108     hex_id[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32 - 1] = '\0';
109     log_debug("Received identity key: %s", hex_id);
110 
111     // DEBUG: Print ephemeral key and signature
112     char hex_eph[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32];
113     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; i++) {
114       safe_snprintf(hex_eph + i * 2, 3, "%02x", server_ephemeral_key[i]);
115     }
116     hex_eph[HEX_STRING_SIZE_32 - 1] = '\0';
117     log_debug("Received ephemeral key: %s", hex_eph);
118 
119     char hex_sig[HEX_STRING_SIZE_64];
120     for (int i = 0; i < ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE; i++) {
121       safe_snprintf(hex_sig + i * 2, 3, "%02x", server_signature[i]);
122     }
123     hex_sig[HEX_STRING_SIZE_64 - 1] = '\0';
124     log_debug("Received signature: %s", hex_sig);
125 
126     // Verify signature: server identity signed the ephemeral key
127     log_debug("Verifying server's signature over ephemeral key (already logged above)");
128 
129     // If client didn't specify --server-key, skip signature verification
130     // (client doesn't care about server identity verification)
131     if (!ctx->verify_server_key) {
132       log_info("Skipping server signature verification (no --server-key specified)");
133       log_warn("Connection is encrypted but server identity is NOT verified (vulnerable to MITM)");
134     } else {
135       // Extract GPG key ID from expected_server_key if it's a GPG key (gpg:KEYID format)
136       const char *gpg_key_id = NULL;
137       if (ctx->expected_server_key[0] != '\0' && strncmp(ctx->expected_server_key, "gpg:", 4) == 0) {
138         const char *key_id_start = ctx->expected_server_key + 4;
139         size_t key_id_len = strlen(key_id_start);
140         // Accept 8, 16, or 40 character GPG key IDs (short, long, or full fingerprint)
141         if (key_id_len == 8 || key_id_len == 16 || key_id_len == 40) {
142           gpg_key_id = key_id_start;
143           log_debug("Using GPG key ID from --server-key for verification: %s", gpg_key_id);
144         }
145       }
146 
147       if (ed25519_verify_signature(server_identity_key, server_ephemeral_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size,
148                                    server_signature, gpg_key_id) != 0) {
149         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
150         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
151         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
152         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server signature verification FAILED - rejecting connection. "
153                                        "This indicates: Server's identity key does not "
154                                        "match its ephemeral key, Potential man-in-the-middle attack, "
155                                        "Corrupted or malicious server");
156       }
157       log_debug("Server signature verified successfully");
158     }
159 
160     // Verify server identity against expected key if --server-key is specified
161     if (ctx->verify_server_key && strlen(ctx->expected_server_key) > 0) {
162       // Parse ALL expected server keys (github:/gitlab: may have multiple keys)
163       public_key_t expected_keys[MAX_CLIENTS];
164       size_t num_expected_keys = 0;
165       if (parse_public_keys(ctx->expected_server_key, expected_keys, &num_expected_keys, MAX_CLIENTS) != 0 ||
166           num_expected_keys == 0) {
167         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
168         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
169         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
170         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG,
171                          "Failed to parse expected server key: %s. Check that "
172                          "--server-key value is valid (ssh-ed25519 "
173                          "format, github:username, or hex)",
174                          ctx->expected_server_key);
175       }
176 
177       // Compare server's IDENTITY key against ALL expected keys (match any one)
178       // This supports users with multiple SSH keys (e.g., different machines)
179       bool key_matched = false;
180       for (size_t i = 0; i < num_expected_keys; i++) {
181         if (sodium_memcmp(server_identity_key, expected_keys[i].key, ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE) == 0) {
182           key_matched = true;
183           log_debug("Server identity key matched expected key %zu/%zu", i + 1, num_expected_keys);
184           break;
185         }
186       }
187 
188       if (!key_matched) {
189         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
190         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
191         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
192         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO,
193                          "Server identity key mismatch - potential MITM attack! "
194                          "Expected key(s) from: %s (checked %zu keys), Server presented a different key "
195                          "than specified with --server-key, DO NOT CONNECT to this "
196                          "server - likely man-in-the-middle attack!",
197                          ctx->expected_server_key, num_expected_keys);
198       }
199       log_info("Server identity key verified against --server-key (%zu key(s) checked)", num_expected_keys);
200     }
201 
202     // Note: Server IP resolution now handled by caller (TCP transport layer)
203     // For WebSocket/WebRTC transports, server_ip should be set by the transport
204     // before handshake begins. TCP clients will use the legacy wrapper which
205     // handles this.
206     if (ctx->server_ip[0] == '\0') {
207       log_debug("Server IP not set - skipping known_hosts verification (non-TCP transport)");
208     } else {
209       log_debug("Server IP already set: %s", ctx->server_ip);
210     }
211 
212     // Check known_hosts for this server (if we have server IP and port)
213     // Check if known_hosts verification should be skipped
214     bool skip_known_hosts = false;
215     const char *env_skip = platform_getenv("ASCII_CHAT_INSECURE_NO_HOST_IDENTITY_CHECK");
216     if (env_skip && strcmp(env_skip, STR_ONE) == 0) {
217       log_warn(
218           "Skipping known_hosts checking for authenticated connection (ASCII_CHAT_INSECURE_NO_HOST_IDENTITY_CHECK=1)");
219       skip_known_hosts = true;
220     }
221 #ifndef NDEBUG
222     // In debug builds, also skip for Claude Code (LLM automation can't do interactive prompts)
223     else if (platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE")) {
224       log_warn("Skipping known_hosts checking (CLAUDECODE set in debug build)");
225       skip_known_hosts = true;
226     }
227 #endif
228 
229     if (!skip_known_hosts && ctx->server_ip[0] != '\0' && ctx->server_port > 0) {
230       asciichat_error_t known_host_result = check_known_host(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, server_identity_key);
231       if (known_host_result == ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION) {
232         // Key mismatch - MITM attack detected! Prompt user for confirmation
233         log_error("SECURITY: Server key does NOT match known_hosts entry!\n"
234                   "This indicates a possible man-in-the-middle attack!");
235         uint8_t stored_key[ZERO_KEY_SIZE] = {0}; // We don't have the stored key easily
236                                                  // accessible, use zeros for now
237         if (!display_mitm_warning(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, stored_key, server_identity_key)) {
238           // User declined to continue - ABORT connection for security          SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
239           SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
240           SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
241           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION,
242                            "SECURITY: Connection aborted - server key mismatch (possible MITM attack)");
243         }
244         // User accepted the risk - continue with connection
245         log_warn("SECURITY WARNING: User accepted MITM risk - continuing with connection");
246       } else if (known_host_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
247         // Unknown host (first connection) - prompt user to verify fingerprint
248         if (!prompt_unknown_host(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, server_identity_key)) {
249           // User declined to add host - ABORT connection          SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
250           SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
251           SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
252           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "User declined to verify unknown host");
253         }
254 
255         // User accepted - add to known_hosts
256         if (add_known_host(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, server_identity_key) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
257           SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
258           SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
259           SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
260           return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG,
261                            "CRITICAL SECURITY ERROR: Failed to create known_hosts "
262                            "file! This is a security vulnerability - the "
263                            "program cannot track known hosts. Please check file "
264                            "permissions and ensure the program can write to: %s",
265                            get_known_hosts_path());
266         }
267         log_debug("Server host added to known_hosts successfully");
268       } else if (known_host_result == 1) {
269         // Key matches - connection is secure!
270         log_info("Server host key verified from known_hosts - connection secure");
271       } else {
272         // Unexpected error code from check_known_host        SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
273         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
274         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
275         return SET_ERRNO(known_host_result, "SECURITY: known_hosts verification failed with error code %d",
276                          known_host_result);
277       }
278     }
279   } else if (payload_len == ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size) {
280     // Simple format: just ephemeral key (no identity key)
281     log_debug("Received simple KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT (%zu bytes) - server has no "
282               "identity key",
283               payload_len);
284     memcpy(server_ephemeral_key, payload, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
285 
286     // Clear identity key and signature for simple format
287     memset(server_identity_key, 0, ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size);
288     memset(server_signature, 0, ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size);
289 
290     // Server is not using client authentication in simple mode
291     ctx->server_uses_client_auth = false;
292 
293     log_debug("Received ephemeral key (simple format)");
294 
295     // SECURITY: For servers without identity keys, we implement a different security model:
296     // 1. Verify IP address matches known_hosts entry
297     // 2. Always require user confirmation (no silent connections)
298     // 3. Store server fingerprint for future verification
299 
300     if (ctx->server_ip[0] == '\0' || ctx->server_port <= 0) {
301       SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
302       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
303       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
304       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Server IP or port not set, cannot check known_hosts");
305     }
306 
307     // Check if this server was previously connected to (IP verification)
308     bool skip_known_hosts = false;
309     asciichat_error_t known_host_result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
310     const char *env_skip_known_hosts_checking = platform_getenv("ASCII_CHAT_INSECURE_NO_HOST_IDENTITY_CHECK");
311     if (env_skip_known_hosts_checking && strcmp(env_skip_known_hosts_checking, STR_ONE) == 0) {
312       log_warn("Skipping known_hosts checking. This is a security vulnerability.");
313       skip_known_hosts = true;
314     }
315 #ifndef NDEBUG
316     // In debug builds, also skip for Claude Code (LLM automation can't do interactive prompts)
317     else if (platform_getenv("CLAUDECODE")) {
318       log_warn("Skipping known_hosts checking (CLAUDECODE set in debug build).");
319       skip_known_hosts = true;
320     }
321 #endif
322     else {
323       known_host_result = check_known_host_no_identity(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port);
324     }
325 
326     if (skip_known_hosts || known_host_result == 1) {
327       // Server IP is known and verified - allow connection without warnings
328       log_info("SECURITY: Server IP %s:%u is known (no-identity entry found) - connection verified", ctx->server_ip,
329                ctx->server_port);
330     } else if (known_host_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
331       // Server IP is unknown - require user confirmation
332       log_warn("SECURITY: Unknown server IP %s:%u with no identity key\n"
333                "This connection is vulnerable to man-in-the-middle attacks\n"
334                "Anyone can intercept your connection and read your data",
335                ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port);
336 
337       if (!prompt_unknown_host_no_identity(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port)) {
338         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
339         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
340         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
341         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "User declined to connect to unknown server without identity key");
342       }
343 
344       // User accepted - add to known_hosts as no-identity entry
345       // For servers without identity keys, pass zero key to indicate no-identity
346       uint8_t zero_key[ZERO_KEY_SIZE] = {0};
347       if (add_known_host(ctx->server_ip, ctx->server_port, zero_key) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
348         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
349         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
350         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
351         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CONFIG,
352                          "CRITICAL SECURITY ERROR: Failed to create known_hosts "
353                          "file! This is a security vulnerability - the "
354                          "program cannot track known hosts. Please check file "
355                          "permissions and ensure the program can write to: %s",
356                          get_known_hosts_path());
357       }
358       log_debug("Server host added to known_hosts successfully");
359     } else if (known_host_result == ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION) {
360       // Server previously had identity key but now has none - potential security issue
361       log_warn("SECURITY: Server previously had identity key but now has none - potential security issue");
362       SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
363       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
364       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
365       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION, "Server key configuration changed - potential security issue");
366     } else {
367       // Other error checking known_hosts (e.g., ERROR_INVALID_PARAM)      SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
368       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
369       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
370       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to verify server IP address");
371     }
372   } else {
373     SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
374     SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
375     SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
376     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
377                      "Invalid KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT size: %zu bytes (expected %zu or "
378                      "%zu). This indicates: Protocol violation "
379                      "or incompatible server version, Potential man-in-the-middle "
380                      "attack, Network corruption",
381                      payload_len, expected_auth_size, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size);
382     // retry
383   }
384 
385   // Set peer's public key (EPHEMERAL X25519) - this also derives the shared secret
386   crypto_result_t crypto_result = crypto_set_peer_public_key(&ctx->crypto_ctx, server_ephemeral_key);
387   if (crypto_result != CRYPTO_OK) {
388     SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
389     SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
390     SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
391     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to set peer public key and derive shared secret: %s",
392                      crypto_result_to_string(crypto_result));
393   }
394 
395   // Determine if client has an identity key
396   bool client_has_identity_key = (ctx->client_private_key.type == KEY_TYPE_ED25519);
397 
398   // Send authenticated response if server has identity key (auth_public_key_size > 0)
399   // OR if server requires client authentication (require_client_auth)
400   // Note: server_uses_client_auth is set when server has identity key, but we should
401   // send authenticated response when server has identity key regardless of client auth requirement
402   bool server_has_identity = (ctx->crypto_ctx.auth_public_key_size > 0 && ctx->crypto_ctx.signature_size > 0);
403   bool server_requires_auth = server_has_identity || ctx->require_client_auth;
404 
405   if (server_requires_auth) {
406     // Send authenticated packet:
407     // [ephemeral:kex_size][identity:auth_size][signature:sig_size][gpg_key_id_len:1][gpg_key_id:0-16]
408     // Use Ed25519 sizes since client has Ed25519 key
409     size_t ed25519_pubkey_size = ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE; // Ed25519 public key is always 32 bytes
410     size_t ed25519_sig_size = ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE;     // Ed25519 signature is always 64 bytes
411     size_t response_size = ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size + ed25519_pubkey_size + ed25519_sig_size;
412 
413     // Check if client has a GPG key ID to send
414     uint8_t gpg_key_id_len = 0;
415     if (ctx->client_gpg_key_id[0] != '\0') {
416       gpg_key_id_len = (uint8_t)strlen(ctx->client_gpg_key_id);
417       if (gpg_key_id_len > 40) {
418         gpg_key_id_len = 40; // Truncate to max length (full fingerprint)
419       }
420       response_size += 1 + gpg_key_id_len; // 1 byte for length + key ID
421     } else {
422       response_size += 1; // Just the length byte (0)
423     }
424 
425     uint8_t *key_response = SAFE_MALLOC(response_size, uint8_t *);
426     size_t offset = 0;
427 
428     // Copy ephemeral key
429     memcpy(key_response + offset, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key,
430            ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size); // X25519 ephemeral for encryption
431     offset += ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size;
432 
433     if (client_has_identity_key) {
434       // Client has identity key - send it with signature
435       memcpy(key_response + offset, ctx->client_private_key.public_key, ed25519_pubkey_size); // Ed25519 identity
436       offset += ed25519_pubkey_size;
437 
438       // Sign ephemeral key with client identity key
439       if (ed25519_sign_message(&ctx->client_private_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size,
440                                key_response + offset) != 0) {
441         SAFE_FREE(key_response);
442         SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
443         SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
444         SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
445         return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_CRYPTO, "Failed to sign client ephemeral key");
446       }
447       offset += ed25519_sig_size;
448     } else {
449       // Client has no identity key - send null identity and null signature
450       memset(key_response + offset, 0, ed25519_pubkey_size); // Null identity
451       offset += ed25519_pubkey_size;
452       memset(key_response + offset, 0, ed25519_sig_size); // Null signature
453       offset += ed25519_sig_size;
454     }
455 
456     // Append GPG key ID length
457     key_response[offset] = gpg_key_id_len;
458     offset += 1;
459 
460     // Append GPG key ID if present
461     if (gpg_key_id_len > 0) {
462       memcpy(key_response + offset, ctx->client_gpg_key_id, gpg_key_id_len);
463       offset += gpg_key_id_len;
464       log_debug("Including client GPG key ID in KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE: %.*s", gpg_key_id_len, ctx->client_gpg_key_id);
465     }
466 
467     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP, key_response, response_size, 0);
468     if (result != 0) {
469       SAFE_FREE(key_response);
470       SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
471       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
472       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
473       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE packet");
474     }
475 
476     // Zero out the buffer before freeing
477     sodium_memzero(key_response, response_size);
478     SAFE_FREE(key_response);
479   } else {
480     // Send X25519 encryption key only to server (no identity key)
481     // Format: [X25519 pubkey (kex_size)] = kex_size bytes total
482     result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP, ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key,
483                                        ctx->crypto_ctx.public_key_size, 0);
484     if (result != 0) {
485       SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
486       SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
487       SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
488       return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to send KEY_EXCHANGE_RESPONSE packet");
489     }
490   }
491 
492   ctx->state = CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE;
493 
494   // Free temporary buffers before successful return
495   SAFE_FREE(server_ephemeral_key);
496   SAFE_FREE(server_identity_key);
497   SAFE_FREE(server_signature);
498 
499   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
500 }






References add_known_host(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_context_t::auth_public_key_size, check_known_host(),
    check_known_host_no_identity(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_gpg_key_id,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::client_private_key,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::crypto_ctx, CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_INIT,
    CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE_KEY_EXCHANGE,
    crypto_handshake_validate_packet_size(), CRYPTO_OK,
    crypto_result_to_string(), crypto_set_peer_public_key(),
    display_mitm_warning(), ED25519_PUBLIC_KEY_SIZE,
    ed25519_sign_message(), ED25519_SIGNATURE_SIZE,
    ed25519_verify_signature(), ERROR_CONFIG, ERROR_CRYPTO,
    ERROR_CRYPTO_VERIFICATION, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    ERROR_NETWORK_PROTOCOL,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::expected_server_key,
    get_known_hosts_path(), HEX_STRING_SIZE_32,
    HEX_STRING_SIZE_64, KEY_TYPE_ED25519, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn, MAX_CLIENTS,
    packet_send_via_transport(),
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT,
    PACKET_TYPE_CRYPTO_KEY_EXCHANGE_RESP, parse_public_keys(),
    platform_getenv(), prompt_unknown_host(),
    prompt_unknown_host_no_identity(),
    crypto_context_t::public_key, private_key_t::public_key,
    crypto_context_t::public_key_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::require_client_auth, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, safe_snprintf(),
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_ip,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_port,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::server_uses_client_auth,
    SET_ERRNO, crypto_context_t::signature_size,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::state, STR_ONE,
    private_key_t::type,
    crypto_handshake_context_t::verify_server_key, and
    ZERO_KEY_SIZE.


Referenced by client_handle_key_exchange_init(), and
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket().






asciichat_error_t
  crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange_socket (crypto_handshake_context_t
  * ctx, socket_t client_socket)


Legacy wrapper: Key exchange using socket (TCP clients only).


Deprecated


Use crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange() with
  acip_transport_t instead


Definition at line 875 of file
    lib/crypto/handshake/client.c.


875                                                                                                                        {
876   if (!ctx) {
877     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Context cannot be NULL");
878   }
879 
880   // Receive packet using old method
881   packet_type_t packet_type;
882   uint8_t *payload = NULL;
883   size_t payload_len = 0;
884   int result = receive_packet(client_socket, &packet_type, (void **)&payload, &payload_len);
885   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
886     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to receive KEY_EXCHANGE_INIT packet");
887   }
888 
889   // Create temporary TCP transport
890   acip_transport_t *temp_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("crypto_handshake_temp_socket", client_socket, NULL);
891   if (!temp_transport) {
892     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create temporary transport");
893   }
894 
895   // Call new function (takes ownership of payload and will free it)
896   asciichat_error_t handshake_result =
897       crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(ctx, temp_transport, packet_type, payload, payload_len);
898 
899   // Destroy temporary transport
900   acip_transport_destroy(temp_transport);
901 
902   return handshake_result;
903 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(), ASCIICHAT_OK,
    crypto_handshake_client_key_exchange(), ERROR_INVALID_STATE,
    ERROR_NETWORK, receive_packet(), and SET_ERRNO.
